---
title: "အမည်ပေး ကင်ပွန်းတပ် ဖိတ်ကြားလွှာ"
author: "RQuesT"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      comment = "")
```


```{r library, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r waffleUndiagnose, include=TRUE}
tibble(x = rep(1:10, each = 10), y = rep(1:10, times =10)) |> 
    ggplot()+
    geom_point(mapping = aes(x,y, fill = (x>7)),shape = 22, size = 13)+
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1)+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10)+
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:10)+
    scale_fill_brewer(type = "div", palette = 2, 
                      breaks = c(FALSE), 
                      labels = c("70% of individuals with the condition remained undiagnosed due to clinical misconceptions"))+
    theme_void()+
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.title = element_blank(),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.5,units = "lines")
          )
```

```{r risk, include=TRUE}
dt_risk <- tibble(
    x = factor(c("Classic","Non polycystic","Ovulatory","Non-androgenic"),
               levels = c("Classic","Non polycystic","Ovulatory","Non-androgenic")),
    y = factor(c("Highest","High","Moderate","Lower"), levels = c("Lower","Moderate","High","Highest")),
    z = factor(c("Very high (85%)","High","Moderate","Lower"), levels = c("Lower","Moderate","High","Very high (85%)"))
) 
bar1 <- ggplot(dt_risk)+
    geom_col(aes(x, as.numeric(y),fill = x == "Non polycystic"))+
    scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = 2, name = "", breaks = NULL)+
    scale_y_continuous(name = "",breaks = NULL, limits = c(0,5))+
    geom_text(aes(x, as.numeric(y), label = y), nudge_y = 0.15)+
    theme_bw()+
    theme(axis.title = element_blank())+
    labs(title = "Metabolic risk of the condition")

bar2 <- ggplot(dt_risk)+
    geom_col(aes(x, as.numeric(z),fill = !(x == "Non polycystic")))+
    scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = 6, name = "", breaks = NULL)+
    scale_y_continuous(name = "",breaks = NULL, limits = c(0,5))+
    geom_text(aes(x, as.numeric(z), label = z), nudge_y = 0.15, nudge_x = 0.1)+
    theme_bw()+
    theme(axis.title = element_blank())+
    labs(title = "Insulin resistance prevalence")

bar1 + bar2
```

```{r timeline, include=TRUE}
timeline <- tibble(
    Year = factor(c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"), 
                  levels = c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"),
                  ordered = T),
    event = c("NIH recommended renaming","Failed efforts","Supports from patients and professionals",
              "First global survey","First nominal group process","Second global survey","Second nominal group process","Formal announcemnt and publication"),
    step = 1:8
)
ggplot(timeline, aes(x = 1, y = Year)) +
  # Draws the central timeline spine connecting the periods
  geom_path(aes(group = 1), color = "#007F7F", linewidth = 1) +
  
  # Adds milestone points at each year/date
  geom_point(color = "#007F7F", size = 5) +
  
  # Adds the event descriptions, left-aligned (hjust = 0) and slightly nudged to the right
  geom_text(aes(label = event), color = "#007F7F",hjust = 1, nudge_x = 0.01, size = 3.8) +
  
  # Reverses the Y-axis levels to read chronologically from top to bottom
  #scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(timeline$Year))) +
  
  # Adds padding to the right side (60% expansion) to prevent text cutoff
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.6))) +
  
  # Title and Clean Axis Labels
  labs(
    title = "Event Timeline",
    x = NULL, 
    y = NULL
  ) +
  
  # Apply Minimal Theme and customize details
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Remove x-axis text/ticks since all events are aligned vertically at x=1
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    
    # Style the Year labels on the left side
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 11),
    
    # Strip away background gridlines to maximize contrast
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # Add a clean title margin
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, margin = margin(b = 15))
  )
```

```{r xn1, include=FALSE}

# 1. Define and prepare the dataset with alternating positioning
timeline <- tibble(
  Year = factor(c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"), 
                levels = c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"),
                ordered = TRUE),
  event = c("NIH recommended renaming","Failed efforts","Supports from patients and professionals",
            "First global survey","First nominal group process","Second global survey","Second nominal group process","Formal announcemnt and publication")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    # Create an index row number to alternate positions
    id = row_number(),
    # If odd, place on the left (-0.5); if even, place on the right (0.5)
    x_pos = if_else(id %% 2 == 1, -0.5, 0.5),
    # If odd, right-align text (1); if even, left-align text (0)
    text_align = if_else(id %% 2 == 1, 1, 0),
    # Add a tiny buffer so text doesn't overlap the end of the ladder rung
    text_pos = if_else(id %% 2 == 1, x_pos - 0.05, x_pos + 0.05)
  )

# 2. Build the Step-Ladder Timeline
ggplot(timeline, aes(y = Year)) +
  # Central Spine (The trunk of the ladder at x = 0)
  geom_path(aes(x = 0, group = 1), color = "grey60", linewidth = 1.5) +
  
  # Central Dots (Milestones on the main trunk)
  geom_point(aes(x = 0), color = "#1f77b4", size = 3.5) +
  
  # Ladder Rungs (Horizontal branches extending out to the sides)
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = x_pos, yend = Year), color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Event Labels (Positioned at the ends of the rungs)
  geom_text(aes(x = text_pos, label = event, hjust = text_align), size = 3, fontface = "plain") +
  
  # Format X axis to create equal breathing room on both sides of the center line (0)
  xlim(-3.5, 3.5) +
  
  # Reverse the Y axis so the earliest dates start at the top
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(levels(timeline$Year))) +
  
  # Theme and Design customizers
  labs(title = "Project Milestone Timeline", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Remove all axis lines, ticks, and text coordinates except the Year labels
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    
    # Place the Year labels directly over the spine points by styling the axis
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 20))
  )
```
```{r xn2, include=FALSE}

# 1. Prepare data with a numerical 'step' variable
timeline <- tibble(
  Year = factor(c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"), 
                levels = c("2012","2013-2022","2023","April 2025", "November 2025","January 2026","February 2026","May 2026"),
                ordered = TRUE),
  event = c("NIH recommended renaming","Failed efforts","Supports from patients and professionals",
            "First global survey","First nominal group process","Second global survey","Second nominal group process","Formal announcemnt and publication")
) %>% 
  mutate(
    step = row_number()
  )

# 2. Build the Staircase with labels INSIDE and BELOW the steps
ggplot(timeline) +
  # Connects the steps using a clean right-angle line path
  geom_step(aes(x = Year, y = step, group = 1), color = "grey75", linewidth = 1.2, direction = "hv") +
  
  # Your completed geom_label placed tucked inside the step
  geom_label(
    aes(x = Year, y = step, label = event),
    fill = "white",          
    color = "#2c3e50",       
    label.padding = unit(0.3, "lines"), 
    label.size = 0.25,       
    size = 3.5,              
    fontface = "bold",       
    hjust = 0,               # Left-align text so it stretches to the right INSIDE the step
    vjust = 1.5,             # Position it cleanly BELOW the horizontal line
    nudge_x = 0.05           # Slightly nudge it right so it doesn't clip the vertical wall
  ) +
  
  # Add padding to the bottom and right edges so nothing gets clipped in RMD HTML
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.2, 0.1))) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.3))) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Project Milestones & Timeline",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 10, angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 15))
  )
```


```{r nomenclature, include=TRUE}
concensus <- tibble(
    x = c(0.86,0.76),
    y = c("Patients","Health professional")
)
ggplot(concensus)+
    geom_col(aes(x,y), fill = "#007F7F" )+
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0,1), labels = scales::percent_format(),
                       name = "")+
    geom_text(aes(x,y, label = scales::percent(x)), nudge_x = 0.05, colour = "#007F7F")+
    theme_classic()+
    theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey", linewidth = 0.5))+
    labs(y = "",
         title = "Unprecedented global concensus mandates a nomenclature shift",
         subtitle = "Results of a 14 years process involving 56 organizations and over 22,000 respondents \nacross 6 continents. Published in the Lancet (May 2026)")
```
